sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data_column XML, value INT);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, data_column, value) VALUES (1, '<root><item>Value1</item></root>', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, data_column, value) VALUES (2, '<root><item>Value2</item></root>', 100);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, data_column, value) VALUES (3, '<root><item>Value3</item><item>Value4</item></root>', 50);
SELECT id, value, xpath_split(data_column, '<item>') AS split_items FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data AS inner_query WHERE inner_query.value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count, stat_export() AS stats FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING value <= 100;
SELECT MAX(value) AS max_value, stat_export() AS stats FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND length(data_column) > 0) AS filtered_data GROUP BY id;
CREATE TABLE stats_table AS SELECT id, xpath_split(data_column, '<item>') AS split_items FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO stats_table (id, split_items) SELECT id, xpath_split(data_column, '<item>') FROM test_data WHERE data_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT value FROM test_data AS inner WHERE inner.id = outer.id ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 1) AS highest_value FROM test_data AS outer;
SELECT id, stat_export() AS statistics FROM (SELECT id, data_column, xpath_split(data_column, '<item>') FROM test_data) AS derived_data GROUP BY id;
